chapter_title.en: Chapter 32 – Marriage of the Zimmy woman (under the responsibility)

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/65)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف على المشهور - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (0/65)
text: سَأَلْتُ أَبَا جَعْفَرٍ ( عليه السلام ) أَ يَتَزَوَّجُ الْمَجُوسِيَّةَ قَالَ لَا وَ لَكِنْ إِنْ كَانَتْ لَهُ أَمَةً .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Al Hassan Bin Mahboub, from Al A’ala Bin Razeyn, from Muhammad Bin Muslim who said,
‘I asked Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup>, ‘Can one marry a Magian woman?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘No, but if there was such a slave girl for him’.
en.sarwar: 3. A number of our people have narrated from Sahl ibn Ziyad from al-Hassan ibn Mahbub from al-‘Ala’ ibn Razin from Muhammad ibn Muslim who has said the following: “I once asked abu Ja‘far, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if it is lawful to marry a Zoroastrian woman. He (the Imam) said, ‘No, however, she can be his slave-girl.’”
